common.disclaimer: Calculations are provided for informational purposes only and do not constitute legal, tax, or valuation advice.
common.suggestionText: Tool suggestion, error report, question — we respond.

home.heroDescription: Valuation, energy simulation, market data and calculation tools for Luxembourg real estate. Homeowners, valuers, banks, property managers — all in one place.
home.missingToolDescription: tevaxia.lu is under active development. If you need a specific calculator, a feature for your professional practice, or if you find an error in our calculations — let us know.

aides.title: Subsidies Simulator

achatLocation.title: Buy or Rent?

bilanPromoteur.title: Developer Feasibility
bilanPromoteur.description: Residual method: sale price − costs − fees − margin = maximum land cost. Adapted to Luxembourg construction costs.

calculateurVrd.title: VRD Estimator

home.heroDescription: Simulators dedicated to the energy transition of Luxembourg real estate. Measure the impact, estimate the ROI, explore energy communities.
home.impactDesc: Quantify the green premium / brown discount for each energy class (A to I). Comparative table, methodology and sources.
home.renovationDesc: Costs per work item, Klimabonus (25-62.5%), Klimaprêt, energy savings, NPV, IRR and payback period.
home.communauteDesc: Shared PV production, installation cost, savings per participant, monthly output, CO₂ avoided and regulatory compliance checklist.
home.epbdDesc: European Energy Performance of Buildings Directive. Key deadlines 2026-2050, stranding risk by class, long-term value impact.
home.estimateurDesc: Don't know your energy class? Answer 6 questions to get an indicative estimate of your energy passport rating.
home.lenozDesc: Assess your property's sustainability across the 6 categories of Luxembourg's LENOZ certification. Bronze to Platinum rating.
home.portfolioDesc: Analyse the energy profile of your property portfolio. Multi-property comparison, EPBD risk, weighted average score.
home.hvacDesc: Heating and ventilation sizing per EN 12831. Heat pump catalogue (Viessmann, Daikin, Vaillant...), HRV, pellet boilers. 7-lot bill of quantities, Klimabonus 2026.
home.whyDesc: In Luxembourg, a property's energy class directly influences its market value. The gap between an A-rated and an I-rated property can reach {pct}. With the EPBD directive and rising energy costs, renovation is no longer optional — it's a measurable value driver.

impact.title: Energy class impact on property value
impact.description: Visualize how energy performance influences property prices in Luxembourg.
impact.source: Source: Observatoire de l'Habitat, Spuerkeess analyses
impact.apiConnecting: Connecting to calculation server... First load may take up to 60 seconds.
impact.localFallback: Local calculation — Spring Boot API not connected
impact.explainTitle: What is the green premium / brown discount?
impact.explainGreenText: Energy-efficient properties (classes A to C) sell above the market average. This premium reflects lower heating costs, superior thermal comfort and early compliance with future EPBD standards. In Luxembourg, Observatoire de l'Habitat studies show a premium of up to +8% for A-rated properties relative to the D reference class.
impact.explainBrownText: Conversely, energy-inefficient properties (classes E to I) suffer a discount. Buyers anticipate future renovation costs, rising energy prices and increasing regulatory constraints (progressive rental bans on energy-wasting buildings, EPBD directive 2024). The discount can reach -25% for I-rated properties.
impact.explainMethodText: Adjustment percentages are calibrated from observed transaction price gaps in Luxembourg, segmented by energy passport class. Class D serves as the reference (0% adjustment). Values are consistent with European academic studies and ECB climate risk analyses.
impact.explainSource1: Observatoire de l'Habitat du Luxembourg — Sales price analysis by energy class (2023-2025)
impact.explainSource2: ECB — Climate risk and real estate: emerging evidence on pricing (2024)
impact.explainSource3: EPBD recast Directive 2024/1275 — Zero-emission building stock by 2050
impact.explainSource4: TEGOVA EVS 2025 — ESG & Sustainability in Property Valuation (EVS 5)
impact.fourchetteTip: Variations depend on location, property type and market conditions
impact.calibrationNote: Coefficients calibrated on price differences by energy class observed in Luxembourg (Observatoire de l'Habitat 2025) and European studies (ECB, PriceHubble).

renovation.title: Energy Renovation ROI
renovation.description: Estimate the cost, value gain and return on investment of an energy renovation.
renovation.aucunPoste: No renovation items identified for this class upgrade.
renovation.vanDesc: Net present value (3% discount rate, 3%/year energy price increase)
renovation.apiConnecting: Connecting to calculation server... First load may take up to 60 seconds.
renovation.localFallback: Local calculation — Spring Boot API not connected
renovation.roiDesc: Value gain / net cost after subsidies
renovation.klimapretEcoDesc: Klimaprêt payment: {mens} €/mo vs {mensMarche} €/mo at market rate
renovation.klimapretZeroCash: Zero upfront cash — Klimaprêt finances the full remaining cost

communaute.title: Energy Community Simulator
communaute.description: Estimate the savings and benefits of a renewable energy community between neighbours or co-owners.
communaute.paramAutoconso: Base self-consumption rate: {base}% + {foisonnement}% per participant (diversity effect)
communaute.paramCO2: CO₂ factor Luxembourg electricity mix: {val} g/kWh
communaute.coutBase: Based on €1,200/kWp ex-VAT (PV modules, inverter, wiring, installation). Luxembourg market 2026.
communaute.prodMensDesc: Distribution based on average solar irradiance in Luxembourg (south-facing, 35° tilt)
communaute.apiConnecting: Connecting to calculation server... First load may take up to 60 seconds.
communaute.localFallback: Local calculation — Spring Boot API not connected

epbd.title: EPBD Directive — Timeline & Stranding Risk
epbd.description: The European Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (EPBD recast 2024) sets a decarbonisation timeline for the building stock. Select your property's energy class to see the deadlines that apply and assess your stranding risk.
epbd.selectorLabel: Current energy class of your property
epbd.projectionGreenPremium: Green premium projection if class remains unchanged.
epbd.projectionBrownDiscount: Brown discount projection if no renovation is undertaken.
epbd.projectionDisclaimer: Indicative projections based on observed trends and the EPBD timeline. Not financial advice.
epbd.renovationCtaDescription: Estimate the cost, subsidies (Klimabonus, Klimaprêt) and return on investment of an energy renovation.
epbd.source1: Directive (EU) 2024/1275 of 24 April 2024 on the energy performance of buildings (recast)
epbd.source2: Official Journal of the European Union, L 2024/1275, 8 May 2024
epbd.source3: European Commission — Fit for 55, Renovation Wave Strategy
epbd.source4: Ministry of Energy and Spatial Planning, Luxembourg
epbd.milestone2026Detail: Deadline: 29 May 2026. Each member state must transpose the directive into national law.
epbd.milestone2028Detail: All new public buildings must be zero-emission from 2028.
epbd.milestone2030Detail: 16% reduction in primary energy consumption of the worst performing residential buildings.
epbd.milestone2033Detail: 26% reduction in primary energy consumption. Classes E-I must have undergone significant renovations.
epbd.milestone2035Detail: Zero-emission requirement for all new buildings (public and private).
epbd.milestone2040Detail: Total ban on fossil fuel boilers (gas, oil).
epbd.milestone2050Detail: Target: the entire European building stock reaches net zero emissions.

riskLow.description: Your property complies with 2050 targets. Value preserved or enhanced (green premium).
riskLow.action2: Consider improvements to maximise green premium
riskLow.valueImpact: Stable to positive. Growing green premium (+2 to +8% vs class D).

riskModerate.description: Class D: not a 'worst performer', but affected by the fossil boiler ban (2040) and zero-emission target (2050).
riskModerate.action1: Plan a renovation to class B or A before 2035
riskModerate.action2: Replace fossil boiler with a heat pump
riskModerate.action3: Take advantage of Klimabonus while subsidies are available
riskModerate.action4: Anticipate growing discount on mid-range classes
riskModerate.valueImpact: Progressive discount expected. By 2040, -8 to -12% without renovation.

riskHigh.description: Classes E-F: directly affected by the 2030-2033 reduction targets. Risk of non-compliance and significant discount.
riskHigh.action1: Start a deep energy renovation promptly
riskHigh.action2: Target at least class C, ideally B or A
riskHigh.action4: Maximise Klimabonus subsidies (higher rates for bigger class jumps)
riskHigh.valueImpact: Significant discount. By 2033, -10 to -18% without renovation.

riskCritical.description: Classes G-I: 'worst performer' impacted from 2030. Discount already visible and accelerating.
riskCritical.action2: Target class B or A to maximise ROI
riskCritical.action6: If selling: expect aggressive buyer negotiation
riskCritical.valueImpact: Critical discount. Current brown discount -12 to -25%. By 2030, potentially > -30%.

estimateurCpe.title: Estimate your energy class
estimateurCpe.description: Answer 6 simple questions to get an indicative estimate of the energy class (CPE) of your property in Luxembourg.
estimateurCpe.selectInstruction: Select the answer that best matches your property.
estimateurCpe.estimatedClassLabel: Estimated energy class of your property
estimateurCpe.disclaimerText: Only an official energy passport (CPE) issued by an accredited expert is legally binding.
estimateurCpe.exploreTitle: Explore simulators with your estimated class
estimateurCpe.exploreDescription: Class {classe} automatically selected in each simulator.

lenoz.title: Simplified LENOZ Scoring
lenoz.description: LENOZ is Luxembourg's sustainability certification for residential buildings. The official version includes 143 criteria across 6 categories. This simulator offers a simplified grid of approximately 20 key criteria for a quick pre-assessment.
lenoz.disclaimerText: Simplified estimate — the official LENOZ certification requires a full audit by an accredited body (143 criteria). This simulator provides a preliminary indication and does not replace the official process.

portfolio.title: Energy Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: Analyse the energy profile of your property portfolio
portfolio.emptyDescription: Add your first property to start analysing the energy profile of your portfolio.
portfolio.ifAllClassB: If the entire portfolio reaches class B
portfolio.epbdWarning: {worst} of {total} properties are worst performers and must be renovated before 2033
portfolio.epbdDescription: The EPBD directive requires renovation of the least performing buildings (classes F, G, H, I) before 2033.
portfolio.csvErrorFormat: Invalid CSV format. Check columns: name, class, area, value, type, year.

estimation.title: Property Estimation
estimation.subtitle: Data-driven estimation using Luxembourg public data
estimation.neufHint: VEFA reference price instead of existing
estimation.comparaisonExplication: Transactions reflect prices actually paid (notarial deeds). Listings reflect asking prices (often higher). The gap is an indicator of the negotiation margin.
estimation.margeNegociationDetail: Listed prices are on average {pct}% higher than prices actually paid (notarial deeds) in this municipality. This gap indicates the possible negotiation margin.
estimation.chargesExplication: Co-ownership charges ~€250/month for an apartment in Luxembourg. Property tax very low in LU (~€15/month).
estimation.renovationDisclaimer: Indicative ranges — Luxembourg market. Eligible for Klimabonus (up to 62.5%).
estimation.disclaimer: Indicative estimate based on public data from the Observatoire de l'Habitat and statistical adjustment coefficients. This is not a professional valuation. For an EVS 2025 compliant appraisal, use the
estimation.selectCommune: Select a municipality to get an estimate
estimation.quartierSource: Indicative prices by neighbourhood — Source: aggregated listings

fraisAcquisition.title: Acquisition Fees Simulator
fraisAcquisition.subtitle: Registration duties, Bëllegen Akt, VAT, notarial fees, mortgage fees
fraisAcquisition.residencePrincipaleHint: Eligible for Bëllegen Akt and 3% VAT
fraisAcquisition.infobellegenText: Tax credit of €40,000 per buyer (€80,000 for a couple) on registration duties. Only applicable for main residence and first use.
fraisAcquisition.infoVefaText: The 7% duties only apply to the land portion. The construction portion is subject to VAT (3% main residence, 17% otherwise), with a tax benefit cap of €50,000.
fraisAcquisition.infoNotaireText: Calculated according to a regulated degressive scale (4% up to €10,000 then degressive).

plusValues.title: Capital Gains Tax Calculator
plusValues.subtitle: Art. 99ter (speculation) and Art. 99bis / 102 LIR — STATEC revaluation coefficients
plusValues.legendeCession: > 2 years: Disposal — half rate + STATEC revaluation
plusValues.infoSpeculationText: Taxed at the full rate (progressive scale). No revaluation of the acquisition price.
plusValues.infoCessionText: Acquisition price revalued using STATEC coefficients. Decennial allowance of €50,000 (€100,000 for couples). Taxed at the half rate.
plusValues.infoResidenceText: Full exemption if effectively and continuously occupied.
plusValues.successionHint: Value used in the succession declaration
plusValues.donationHint: Value used in the notarial deed of donation

calculLoyer.title: Rent Calculator — Invested Capital
calculLoyer.subtitle: Calculate the maximum legal rent under the law of 21 September 2006 on residential leases in Luxembourg.
calculLoyer.prixAcquisitionHint: Purchase price as stated in the notarial deed
calculLoyer.appliquerVetusteHint: Reduces invested capital based on the property age
calculLoyer.vetusteWarningText: Depreciation is not provided for by the 2006 law but may be invoked by the tenant. Application is at the discretion of the justice of the peace.
calculLoyer.loi2006Text: The annual rent may not exceed 5% of the invested capital revalued using STATEC revaluation coefficients.
calculLoyer.capitalInvestiText: revalued acquisition price + revalued improvement works.
calculLoyer.reforme2024Text: The threshold was lowered from 5% to 3.5% for dwellings whose invested capital exceeds a certain ceiling. Please check the applicable conditions.

achatLocation.title: Buy or Rent in Luxembourg
achatLocation.subtitle: Compare the evolution of your net wealth depending on whether you buy or rent your primary residence.
achatLocation.fraisAcquisitionHint: Registration duties, notary fees, VAT as applicable
achatLocation.entretienAnnuelHint: Annual maintenance budget (1% of price is a standard estimate)
achatLocation.tauxAssuranceSRDHint: Mandatory in Luxembourg. Rate between 0.15% and 0.40% of borrowed capital.
achatLocation.deductionInteretsDescription: Art. 98bis LIR: mortgage interest is deductible from taxable income (ceiling per person and per bracket).
achatLocation.tauxMarginalHint: Marginal income tax bracket. Class 1/1a: up to 42%, class 2: joint schedule.
achatLocation.indexationLoyerHint: Estimated annual rent increase (LU health index)
achatLocation.rendementPlacementHint: If you rent, the down payment is invested. Expected annual return.
achatLocation.verdictAchat: Over {horizon} years, buying is more advantageous
achatLocation.verdictLocation: Over {horizon} years, renting is more advantageous
achatLocation.croisementMessage: Buying becomes more advantageous from year {annee}
achatLocation.chartDescription: Net wealth from purchase (property value − outstanding capital) vs accumulated capital from renting (investment of down payment + cost differences).
achatLocation.hypothesesText: Property appreciation: {appreciation}%/yr. Investment return: {rendement}%/yr. Interest deduction ceiling: {plafond}/yr ({nbPersonnes} pers.). Balance insurance: {tauxSRD}% of capital.

simulateurAides.title: Housing Assistance Simulator
simulateurAides.subtitle: Estimate the state, municipal and tax benefits you may be entitled to for your real estate project in Luxembourg.
simulateurAides.montantTravauxHint: Total budget for energy renovation works
simulateurAides.bienNeufHint: Entitles to a tax credit of €20,000 per person
simulateurAides.epargneReguliereHint: Entitles to the housing savings premium and interest bonus
simulateurAides.residenceRequiseText: Most Luxembourg subsidies are conditional on occupancy as primary residence. For rental investments, only mortgage interest deductions and certain depreciations apply.
simulateurAides.estimationsText: The amounts displayed are estimates based on current schedules. The exact amount depends on your personal situation and detailed eligibility conditions.
simulateurAides.aidesCommunalesText: Aid varies considerably from one municipality to another. Here are some examples:
simulateurAides.communeLuxVille: Acquisition premium up to €30,000, rental assistance
simulateurAides.communeBeckerich: Energy model municipality — specific Klimabonus+ premiums
simulateurAides.contactCommune: Contact your municipal administration to find out the exact aid you may be entitled to.
simulateurAides.cumulText: State and municipal aid can generally be combined, up to an overall ceiling (as a rule, the total cannot exceed the total cost of the project).
simulateurAides.topupSocialHint: x1.5 increase of Klimabonus grants if taxable income ≤ €60,000/year (new 2026)

dcfMulti.title: Multi-Tenant DCF
dcfMulti.subtitle: Discounted cash flow valuation for a multi-let investment property.
dcfMulti.capexHint: Recurring capital expenditure (major maintenance, equipment replacement)
dcfMulti.detteHint: For the equity IRR calculation (levered IRR)
dcfMulti.optionBreakHint: Leave empty if no break option
dcfMulti.ervHint: Estimated Rental Value — market rent per m²

carte.title: Property Price Map
carte.subtitle: Sale prices per m² by municipality in Luxembourg — sources: Land Registry, STATEC, listings.
carte.mapHint: Click on a municipality to view details.
carte.demographicsSource: Source: STATEC, National Register of Natural Persons
carte.clickCommune: Click on a municipality to display price details and demographics.

outilsBancaires.title: Banking Tools
outilsBancaires.subtitle: LTV, borrowing capacity, amortisation schedule and DSCR — compliant with Luxembourg banking practices (CRR, CSSF, EBA).
outilsBancaires.ltvStandardDesc: Standard LTV for a mortgage loan in Luxembourg. Required by most banks.
outilsBancaires.ltvFirstBuyerDesc: LTV tolerated for first-time buyers subject to conditions (sufficient income, balance insurance).
outilsBancaires.ltvStateGuaranteeDesc: Beyond 90%, a state guarantee or additional deposit is required.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueIntro: Luxembourg banks use the Mortgage Lending Value (MLV) pursuant to Art. 4(1)(74) CRR and EBA/GL/2020/06 guidelines.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueMethod: The MLV applies prudential discounts (cyclical, marketability, specific) to the market value to obtain a sustainable value.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueLegal: The CSSF requires the LTV ratio to be calculated on the MLV, not on the market value.
outilsBancaires.prudentValueCalculate: Calculate the MLV of your property with our <link>EVS 2025 valuation tool</link>.
outilsBancaires.remainingBalanceInsuranceHint: Mandatory in Luxembourg. Monthly cost added to the payment.
outilsBancaires.indicativeRatesSource: Indicative rates — Spuerkeess, BIL, Raiffeisen, BGL BNP Paribas. March 2025.
outilsBancaires.annualOperatingChargesHint: Non-recoverable landlord charges, maintenance, management, insurance, property tax
outilsBancaires.annualDebtServiceHint: Principal + interest repaid over the year
outilsBancaires.dscrBelow1Desc: Property does not cover the debt service
outilsBancaires.dscr1to1_2Desc: Low margin — risk in case of vacancy
outilsBancaires.dscrAbove1_2Desc: Minimum threshold required by most LU banks
outilsBancaires.tauxMarcheSource: Source: BCL / Switchr.lu — observed average rates
outilsBancaires.tauxNote: Indicative rates — consult your bank for a personalised offer

bilanPromoteur.title: Developer Feasibility
bilanPromoteur.subtitle: Residual land value and feasibility analysis for a real estate development in Luxembourg.
bilanPromoteur.sellingPricePerSqmHint: Average selling price per sellable m²
bilanPromoteur.grossAreaHint: Gross external area (GEA) or floor area
bilanPromoteur.constructionCostPerSqmHint: Works cost per gross m² (€2,500–3,500/m² in Luxembourg)
bilanPromoteur.lotCountHint: Number of lots created by the subdivision
bilanPromoteur.preCommercialisationHint: Share of revenue pre-sold under VEFA before works start
bilanPromoteur.marginOnCAHint: Developer gross margin (10–20% of revenue in Luxembourg)
bilanPromoteur.notProfitableWithLand: The operation is not profitable at this land cost.
bilanPromoteur.negativeLandCharge: The land charge is negative: the project is not viable under these conditions.
bilanPromoteur.methodNote: The developer feasibility is a decision-support tool. Amounts are estimates and do not replace a detailed feasibility study by a construction economist.
bilanPromoteur.treasuryPlanDesc: Quarterly cash flow estimate with {pct}% VEFA pre-sales.
bilanPromoteur.noFinancingNeed: The project is self-financing through VEFA pre-sales.
bilanPromoteur.sensitivityDesc: Residual margin variation under different scenarios. Cells show margin (% of revenue) and resulting land value.

marche.title: Market Database
marche.subtitle: Real estate market data in Luxembourg — residential, offices, retail, logistics, land and macro indicators.

valorisation.pageSubtitle: Value a property according to TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards: comparison, capitalisation, term & reversion, DCF, ESG, energy, MLV, reconciliation.
valorisation.refMarcheSource: Sources: Land Registry, STATEC, Housing Observatory, listings
valorisation.quartiersSourceNote: Indicative neighbourhood prices — sources: listings, transactions, local estimates.
valorisation.ajustezCaracteristiques: Adjust according to your property's characteristics.
valorisation.compCreePrixMoyen: A comparable will be created with the municipal average price. Then adjust the parameters.
valorisation.compGuideText: You can either use a real comparable (a recently sold property whose details you know), or start from a virtual property initialized with the municipal average price. In both cases, adjust each criterion (location, condition, floor, outdoor space, parking, date) relative to the property you are valuing.
valorisation.comparablesSub: Enter comparable sales and adjust characteristics.
valorisation.ajustementsHint: in % — positive = subject property is superior
valorisation.selectCommuneStart: Search a municipality to start the valuation
valorisation.loyerMarcheERVHint: If provided, enables reversionary yield calculation
valorisation.capTauxCapNote: The capitalisation rate reflects the market's expected return. A low rate = high value. Adjust based on segment, location and risk.
valorisation.tauxActualisationHint: WACC or investor's required rate of return
valorisation.tauxCapSortieHint: Cap rate applied at resale at end of period
valorisation.fraisCessionHint: Transfer duties, agent commission (6–8% in Luxembourg)
valorisation.dcfTauxNote: The discount rate and exit yield are the most sensitive DCF parameters. Calibrate them with recent market transactions.
valorisation.dcfSensibiliteNote: The highlighted cell corresponds to the selected parameters.
valorisation.trERVHint: Estimated Rental Value — annual market rent
valorisation.trTauxReversionHint: Yield on market rent — less secure, therefore higher
valorisation.trMethodeNote: The term & reversion method values secured income (term) and future market income (reversion) separately.
valorisation.esgEVSNote: ESG assessment compliant with EVS 5 (TEGOVA EVS 2025) — Sustainability in Property Valuation.
valorisation.enValeurApresHint: Estimated property value once target class is reached
valorisation.enFourchetteNote: Estimate based on Luxembourg statistical data.
valorisation.enTravauxHint: Total amount of energy renovation works
valorisation.enMargePrudentielleHint: Safety margin on total cost (5–15% recommended)
valorisation.enPrincipeText: The residual value after energy renovation is the improved property value minus net works cost and a prudential margin.
valorisation.enFormule: Value after renovation − Net costs − Prudential margin
valorisation.enArt208EBAText: For mortgage lending, the lending value (MLV) must reflect the sustainable value of the property.
valorisation.mlvValeurMarcheEVS1: Market value per EVS 1 (TEGOVA). Derived from the reconciliation of valuation methods.
valorisation.mlvIssueReconciliation: The market value is automatically taken from the Reconciliation tab.
valorisation.mlvCRRNote: The MLV is defined by Art. 4(1)(74) CRR and EBA/GL/2020/06 guidelines.
valorisation.mlvCRR2Subtitle: Regulatory risk weights by loan-to-value ratio (LTV)
valorisation.recScenarioHautHint: Upward variation applied to the reconciled value
valorisation.recScenarioBasHint: Downward variation applied to the reconciled value
valorisation.recScenarioNote: Scenarios allow testing the sensitivity of the value.
valorisation.recScenariosNote: High and low scenarios present a value range in line with TEGOVA EVS 2025 best practice.
valorisation.rptCommentairePlaceholder: Add your observations, supplementary analyses or reservations for this section...
valorisation.rptIndependanceText: The valuer declares that they have no dependency, financial interest or conflict of interest with regard to the property valued, the client or any party involved in the transaction.
valorisation.rptCompetenceText: The valuer has the qualifications, experience and knowledge of the local market required to carry out this assignment in accordance with TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards.
valorisation.rptAutoPopulated: Data automatically populated from the Comparison tab.
valorisation.rptSelectCommuneFirst: First select a municipality in the Comparison tab to automatically populate this section.
valorisation.rptServitudesHint: Rights of way, co-ownership charges, restrictions...
valorisation.rptPAGNote: Consult the PAG (Plan d'Aménagement Général) and PAP (Plan d'Aménagement Particulier) of the municipality to verify the urban planning classification, land use designations and floor area ratios (CUS/COS).
valorisation.rptESGArt208Text: Environmental, social and governance (ESG) factors must be taken into account in accordance with EVS 5 (Sustainability). Art. 208 CRR requires that the sustainable value reflects climate risk and energy performance.
valorisation.rptAutoFromTabs: Data automatically sourced from calculation tabs.
valorisation.rptFillCapOrDCF: Fill in the Capitalisation or DCF tab to populate this section.
valorisation.rptFillAtLeastOneMethod: Complete at least one valuation method (Comparison, Capitalisation or DCF).
valorisation.rptCertificationBody: I certify that this valuation has been carried out in accordance with TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards (10th edition), in full independence and impartiality, and that the conclusions reflect my professional analysis of the market and the property valued.
valorisation.rpt1_1_sirenHint: SIREN number, RCS Luxembourg or legal identifier
valorisation.rpt1_2_finaliteHint: Mortgage guarantee, acquisition, arbitration, accounting balance sheet, succession, etc.
valorisation.rpt1_2_utilisationHint: Investment decision, credit file, IFRS, etc.
valorisation.rpt1_3_title: Identification of valued property or rights
valorisation.rpt1_4_auto: Valuation Charter, 5th ed. + TEGOVA EVS 2025, 10th ed.
valorisation.rpt1_5b_hint: Special assumptions as defined by EVS 5: forced sale, going concern, etc.
valorisation.rpt1_6_hint: Additional details on independence, if applicable
valorisation.rpt1_11_fournisHint: Title deed, lease, co-ownership regulations, GM minutes, diagnostics...
valorisation.rpt1_11_demandesNonFournisHint: Documents requested but not obtained by the report date
valorisation.rpt1_11_collectesHint: Cadastre, PAG, market data, land registry extracts...
valorisation.rpt1_12_inspection: Reservation: limited inspection (some areas not visited)
valorisation.rpt1_12_clausesHint: Other restrictive clauses, liability limitations, specific reservations...
valorisation.rpt2_3_hint: Noise, visual nuisances, quality of neighbouring buildings, green spaces...
valorisation.rpt2_4_hint: Public transport, motorway, parking, train station, airport, cycle paths...
valorisation.rpt3_3_elementsHint: Exclusive use, right to build upwards, common areas...
valorisation.rpt4_1_hint: Details on PAG zoning, PAP designation, COS/CUS, setbacks...
valorisation.rpt4_2_hint: Alignment, overhang, rights of way, pipelines...
valorisation.rpt4_3_hint: Building rights, densification potential, upward extension...
valorisation.rpt4_4_hint: Infrastructure projects, urban renewal, tram, road...
valorisation.rpt5_1_structureHint: Reinforced concrete, masonry, timber frame, steel...
valorisation.rpt5_3_sourceMesuresHint: RICS measurement, surveyor plan, area certificate...
valorisation.rpt5_4_diagnosticsHint: Asbestos, lead, termites, electricity, gas, sanitation...
valorisation.rpt5_5_confortHint: Heating, air conditioning, lift, home automation, fibre...
valorisation.rpt5_5_installationsHint: Heating type, ventilation, hot water, individual meters...
valorisation.rpt5_6_hint: Parking space(s), cellar(s), box, attic, archives, bike room...
valorisation.rpt5_7_obligationsRenovHint: EPBD deadlines, ban on letting class G/F, municipal obligations...
valorisation.rpt5_7_coutsConformiteHint: Estimated works needed to reach the target energy class
valorisation.rpt5_7_contaminationHint: Polluted soils, asbestos, radon, proximity to ICPE...
valorisation.rpt5_7_eauHint: Flood zone, water table, stormwater management...
valorisation.rpt5_7_certificationsHint: LENOZ, BREEAM, HQE, DGNB, PassivHaus, energy label...
valorisation.rpt6_2_vacantsHint: Vacancy duration, causes, estimated market rent for vacant lots...
valorisation.rpt6_3_hint: Charge allocation, non-recoverable charges, works at owner's expense...
valorisation.rpt7_1_tendancesHint: Price trends, transaction volumes, interest rates, economic climate...
valorisation.rpt7_1_offreDemandeHint: Available stock, marketing periods, rental pressure...
valorisation.rpt7_1_perspectivesHint: Expected developments, structural projects, housing policy...
valorisation.rpt9_1_justificationHint: Why these methods are appropriate for the property and the assignment...
valorisation.rpt9_4_hint: Fill in the Energy tab to populate this section.
valorisation.rpt9_6_hint: Fill in the Term & Reversion tab to populate this section.
valorisation.rpt10_2_impactESGHint: Green premium, brown discount, transition costs, physical risks...
valorisation.rpt10_3_hint: Reservations on the conclusions, conditions precedent...
valorisation.rpt10_3b_hint: Uncertainty level, volatility factors, sensitivity to key assumptions...
valorisation.rpt11_cert1: I have carried out this valuation in full independence and impartiality.
valorisation.rpt11_cert2: The facts presented in this report are accurate to the best of my knowledge.
valorisation.rpt11_cert3: The analyses and conclusions are limited to the stated assumptions and conditions.
valorisation.rpt11_cert4: I have no financial interest, present or future, in the property valued.
valorisation.rpt11_cert5: My remuneration is not linked to the result of the valuation.
valorisation.rpt11_cert6: This valuation complies with TEGOVA EVS 2025 standards (10th ed.) and the Valuation Charter (5th ed.).
valorisation.rptA_description: Select the annexes to include in the PDF report generation.
valorisation.rptA_8_esg: A8 — ESG factors (A8.1 Energy performance, A8.2 Physical risks, A8.3 Transition risks)
valorisation.evsMarketValueDesc: The estimated amount for which an asset or liability should exchange on the valuation date between a willing buyer and a willing seller in an arm's length transaction, after proper marketing, wherein the parties had each acted knowledgeably, prudently, and without compulsion.
valorisation.evsMarketRentDesc: The estimated amount for which a property should be leased on the valuation date between a willing lessor and a willing lessee on appropriate terms in an arm's length transaction, after proper marketing.
valorisation.evsMLVDesc: The value of the property as determined by a prudent assessor, taking into account the long-term sustainability of the value, normal and local market conditions, current use and appropriate alternative uses, excluding any speculative elements. CRR Art. 4(1)(74).
valorisation.evsInvestmentValueDesc: The value of an asset for a particular investor or class of investors, for identified investment objectives. May differ from market value.
valorisation.evsFairValueDesc: The price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date. Concept of 'highest and best use'.
valorisation.evsEquitableValueDesc: The estimated price for the transfer of an asset between identified, knowledgeable, and willing parties that reflects the respective interests of those parties.
valorisation.metricLoyerAfficheReelFranchises: Headline rent vs effective rent (after incentives)
valorisation.notesResAppartement: In Luxembourg, the residential market is dominated by the comparison method. Rent is capped at 5% of invested capital (2006 law). Vacancy rates are historically very low (<3%).
valorisation.notesResMaison: Comparison data is limited in Luxembourg — the Housing Observatory does not publish detailed statistics for houses due to insufficient cadastral data. Longer marketing period than apartments.
valorisation.notesImmeubleRapport: Multi-unit buildings are valued primarily on yield. Analyse each unit: passing rent vs ERV, remaining lease term, reversion potential. The 5% cap applies per residential unit. If the building includes a commercial component, value each component separately then aggregate.
valorisation.notesBureaux: Luxembourg City: mature office market segmented CBD / Kirchberg / Cloche d'Or / Gare. Prime rents ~50-55 EUR/m²/month at Kirchberg. Structural vacancy ~5-7%. Analyse incentives reducing effective vs headline rent. Commercial leases 3/6/9 or fixed.
valorisation.notesCommerces: Distinguish high street (Grand-Rue, avenue de la Gare) from retail parks and shopping centres. Zone A rent is the benchmark. Structural e-commerce risk on some segments. Analyse tenant effort ratio (rent / turnover) — above 8-10%, non-renewal risk.
valorisation.notesHotellerie: Hotels are valued on earnings capacity (stabilised EBITDA), not rent. Distinguish real estate from the operating business. Luxembourg: institutional/business market, occupancy ~70-75%. ADR rising. Profits method (EVS) recommended.
valorisation.notesLogistique: Luxembourg: limited logistics stock, concentrated on Eurohub South (Bettembourg/Dudelange). Rents ~6-9 EUR/m²/month. Long leases (6-9 years firm). ESG increasingly important (BREEAM, rooftop solar).
valorisation.notesTerrain: In Luxembourg, a 1 to 6 price ratio exists between land in Luxembourg City and the north. Check PAG/PAP: buildable zone, COS, CTV/CTL servitudes. The developer's residual method deducts land value from selling price minus construction costs and margin.
valorisation.esgRisqueClasseFG: Energy class F/G — regulatory risk (future rental ban)
valorisation.esgRisqueClasseDE: Energy class D/E — insufficient energy performance
valorisation.esgRisqueInondable: Flood zone — insurance risk and depreciation
valorisation.esgRisquePollueLabel: Proximity to contaminated site — environmental risk
valorisation.esgRecoRenovPrioritaire: Priority energy renovation — eligible for Klimabonus (up to 62.5% of works)
valorisation.esgRecoRenovBC: Consider renovation to reach energy class B/C
valorisation.adjDateExplication: Cumulative variation since the sale of the comparable. Positive = market has risen since the sale, comparable underestimates current value.
valorisation.narrIntroduction: The subject of this analysis is a {assetType} with an area of {surface} m², located in {localisation}. The valuation is carried out on the basis of the {evsType} in accordance with the European Valuation Standards 2025 (TEGOVA, 10th edition).
valorisation.narrContexteMarche: The average observed price in the municipality is {prix}/m² for existing apartments (source: Housing Observatory, notarial deeds).
valorisation.narrMarcheVolumeSignificatif: The local market shows a significant transaction volume ({nb} transactions in the last period), which provides good reliability for reference data.
valorisation.narrMarcheVolumeLimite: The local market shows a limited transaction volume ({nb} transactions), which calls for caution in interpreting comparables.
valorisation.narrMethodeComparaison: **Comparison method** — The value comes to {valeur}, i.e. {m2}/m².
valorisation.narrPosInLine: in line with the municipal average ({prix}/m²)
valorisation.narrCapNOI: The net operating income amounts to {noi}, capitalised at a rate of {taux}%.
valorisation.narrCapSousLoue: The property is currently under-rented compared to the market — reversion potential exists at lease renewal.
valorisation.narrCapSurLoue: The property is rented above market — a downward reversion risk exists at lease expiry.
valorisation.narrMethodeDCF: **Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) method** — The value comes to {valeur}, based on a discount rate of {tauxActu}% and an exit yield of {tauxSortie}%.
valorisation.narrDCFIRR: The internal rate of return (IRR) is {irr}%.
valorisation.narrCoherenceExcellente: The methods converge remarkably, which reinforces the reliability of the estimate.
valorisation.narrCoherenceSatisfaisante: The methods show satisfactory consistency, with the spread remaining within acceptable limits.
valorisation.narrCoherenceEcartSignificatif: A significant spread is observed between the methods. The assumptions should be analysed to identify the source of divergence.
valorisation.narrCoherenceEcartImportant: The large spread between methods calls for in-depth analysis. The assumptions of each approach must be reviewed and justified.
valorisation.narrReconciliation: **Reconciled value** — After weighting the methods, the market value is estimated at **{valeur}**, i.e. {m2}/m².
valorisation.narrMLV: **Mortgage Lending Value (MLV)** — After applying prudential discounts in accordance with CRR Art. 229, the mortgage lending value is {mlv}, i.e. {ratio}% of market value.
valorisation.narrESGClasse: **ESG Factors** — The property has energy class {classe}.
valorisation.narrESGBon: The ESG profile is favourable (score {score}/100, level {niveau}), which is an asset in the current regulatory context.
valorisation.narrESGMoyen: The ESG profile is average (score {score}/100, level {niveau}). Energy renovation works could improve the valuation.
valorisation.narrESGInsuffisant: The ESG profile is insufficient (score {score}/100, level {niveau}). The estimated energy discount is {impact}%. Renovation is recommended.
valorisation.narrReserves: This analysis is provided for indicative purposes based on available public data and user-entered parameters. It does not constitute a property valuation within the meaning of EVS 2025 or the Valuation Charter. For an official valuation, please consult a TEGOVA-certified valuer (REV/TRV).

cookie.message: This site uses audience measurement cookies (Google Analytics) to improve your experience. No personal data is collected through the calculators.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: 22 real estate tools + 7 energy simulators. Instant results, PDF export, EVS 2025 report.

free.name: Free
free.f1: 22 real estate tools (estimation, fees, capital gains, rent, subsidies, EVS valuation, DCF, construction cost, VRD, surfaces, etc.)
free.f2: 7 energy simulators (EPC impact, renovation, community, EPBD, EPC, LENOZ, portfolio)

account.f4: Custom profile on reports (name, logo, contact details)
account.f8: Market alerts (new data on your municipalities)
account.f9: Sign in with Google / LinkedIn / Email

pro.name: Pro
pro.f1: Custom PDF/DOCX reports (logo, header, brand guidelines)

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Are the free tools really unlimited?
faq.a1: Yes. All calculators and simulators work without usage limits, without registration and without advertising. You get your results instantly.
faq.q2: Why create an account if it's already free?
faq.a2: An account lets you save your simulations in the cloud (accessible from any device), export professional PDF reports and customise your profile on generated documents.
faq.a3: Your data is hosted in Europe on secure servers (Supabase). We do not sell or share your information. Without an account, nothing leaves your browser.
faq.q4: When will the Pro tier be available?
faq.a4: The Pro tier is under development. Contact us at contact@tevaxia.lu to be notified at launch or to discuss your specific needs.
faq.q5: Can I use tevaxia for official valuation reports?
faq.a5: The tools provide indicative calculations. The Pro tier will include customisable EVS 2025 compliant reports. For a certified valuation report, a qualified valuer is still required.

pricing.launchBanner: Launch phase — the Pro tier is offered for free

propriete.title: 3. Intellectual Property
propriete.p1: All content on this site (texts, calculators, graphics, simulators, source code) is the property of tevaxia.lu and is protected by copyright law.
propriete.p2: Any reproduction, representation, modification or exploitation, even partial, of all or part of the content of this site is prohibited without prior written authorisation.

sources.title: 4. Data Sources
sources.p1: The data used by the calculation and simulation tools comes from open public sources, including:
sources.statec: STATEC (National Institute of Statistics and Economic Studies)
sources.datapublic: data.public.lu (Luxembourg Open Data portal, CC0 licence)
sources.p2: Revaluation coefficients, tax scales and regulatory thresholds are derived from current Luxembourg legislation.

disclaimer.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
disclaimer.p1: The calculation and simulation tools available on tevaxia.lu are provided for informational and indicative purposes only. They do not constitute legal, tax, financial or real estate valuation advice.
disclaimer.p2: Calculator results are estimates based on the scales and regulations in effect at the date of their last update. They are not a substitute for the advice of a qualified professional (notary, lawyer, accountant, certified REV/TRV valuer).
disclaimer.p3: tevaxia.lu accepts no liability for decisions made on the basis of the results of its tools, nor for any errors in calculations or data used.

droit.title: 6. Applicable Law
droit.p1: This site is governed by Luxembourg law. Any dispute relating to the use of the site shall fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg.

navigation.label: Browsing data:
navigation.text: Technical cookies required for the operation of the site. Google Analytics (with prior consent) for audience measurement: pages visited, visit duration, browser type, anonymised IP address.

compte.label: User account:
compte.text: Email address, name (optional), profession (optional), hashed password. You can create an account via email/password or through a third-party service (Google, LinkedIn).

oauth.label: OAuth login (Google, LinkedIn):
oauth.text: Only your email address and name are transmitted by these services. We do not receive your password, contacts, or any other data from your third-party account. Authentication is managed by Supabase Auth via the OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect protocol.

simulations.label: Simulation data:
simulations.text: Data entered in calculators and simulators (amounts, areas, energy parameters, etc.) is stored in Supabase if you are logged in, or in your browser's localStorage otherwise.

profil.label: Valuer profile:
profil.text: Name, company, professional contact details — stored in your browser's localStorage or in Supabase if you are logged in. This data is used to personalise valuation reports.

finalites.title: 3. Purposes of Processing
finalites.fonctionnement: Service operation (calculations, simulations, display of results)
finalites.sauvegarde: Saving simulations and valuations to your account

baseLegale.title: 4. Legal Basis for Processing

contrat.label: Performance of a contract (Art. 6.1.b GDPR):
contrat.text: creation and management of your account, saving your simulations.

baseLegale.retrait: You may withdraw your consent at any time for processing based on this legal basis.

duree.title: 6. Data Retention
duree.compte: Account data: retained until the account is deleted by the user.
duree.simulations: Simulation data: retained for 2 years from the last activity.

droits.title: 7. Your Rights
droits.intro: In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Luxembourg law of 1 August 2018, you have the following rights:
droits.acces: Right of access to your personal data
droits.effacement: Right to erasure ('right to be forgotten')
droits.exercer: To exercise these rights, contact us at

techniques.label: Technical cookies (necessary):
techniques.text: essential for the operation of the site (language preferences, session). No consent required.

analytiques.label: Analytical cookies (consent):
analytiques.text: Google Analytics, only set after explicit consent. Enable anonymised audience measurement.

auth.label: Authentication cookies:
auth.text: Supabase session management (login token). Required for user account functionality.

transferts.title: 9. Data Transfers Outside the European Union
transferts.p1: Some of our sub-processors are located in the United States. These transfers are governed by standard contractual clauses approved by the European Commission and/or the EU-US Data Privacy Framework adequacy decision.
transferts.p2: Account and simulation data are hosted by Supabase on servers located in the European Union (AWS eu-central-1, Frankfurt, Germany).

securite.title: 10. Security Measures
securite.intro: We implement the following technical and organisational measures to protect your data:
securite.rls: Row Level Security (RLS) on Supabase: each user can only access their own data
securite.hash: Hashed passwords (never stored in plain text)

dpo.title: 11. Data Protection Officer / Contact
dpo.text: For any questions regarding the protection of your personal data, you can contact us at

reclamation.title: 12. Right to Lodge a Complaint
reclamation.text: If you believe that the processing of your personal data constitutes a violation of the GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Luxembourg supervisory authority:

comparer.title: Compare Properties
comparer.subtitle: Estimate and compare properties in Luxembourg — live or from your saved evaluations.
comparer.noSavedHint: Save evaluations from the tools (estimation, valuation, fees...) to compare them here.

comparer.selectAtLeast2: Select at least 2 evaluations to show the comparison.
comparer.disclaimer: Indicative estimate based on public data from the Observatoire de l'Habitat and statistical adjustment coefficients. This is not a professional valuation.

convertisseurSurfaces.title: Surface Area Converter
convertisseurSurfaces.subtitle: Conversion between SCB, SCP, usable area and habitable area — OAI FC.04 and ILNAS 101:2016 standards
convertisseurSurfaces.typeSurfaceHint: Surface from which to calculate the others
convertisseurSurfaces.nbNiveauxHint: Number of above-ground floors of the building
convertisseurSurfaces.mursExterieursHint: Average thickness of facade walls (including insulation)
convertisseurSurfaces.mursPorteursHint: Average thickness of load-bearing walls and shear walls
convertisseurSurfaces.partiesCommunesHint: Halls, stairs, plant rooms — as % of SCP (apartment buildings only)
convertisseurSurfaces.typeBatimentHint: Influences the SCB → SCP ratio (facade perimeter vs area)
convertisseurSurfaces.hauteurMinHint: Minimum ceiling height for habitable surface (ILNAS 101)
convertisseurSurfaces.pctSousHauteurHint: Share of usable area below minimum height (attics, sloped ceilings)
convertisseurSurfaces.prixM2Hint: Average selling price per saleable m²
convertisseurSurfaces.sourcesText: OAI FC.04 (Jan. 2025) — Surface calculation sheets, Order of Architects and Consulting Engineers. ILNAS 101:2016 — Luxembourg standard for habitable surface measurement. ACT — Practical guide to vertical cadastre (Jul. 2023), Administration of Cadastre and Topography. RGD PAG art. 32 — Definition of Gross Built Surface.

estimateurConstruction.sectionCorpsMetierHint: Adjust each item to match your project. Default values correspond to the selected finish level.

estimateurConstruction.tradeDescMenuiserieExt: Windows, exterior doors, shutters, sliding doors
estimateurConstruction.tradeDescChauffage: Heat pump, radiators, underfloor heating, MVHR

calculateurVrd.title: VRD Estimator — Engineering Office
calculateurVrd.subtitle: Quantities, budget estimate and summary schedule — Luxembourg infrastructure projects

steps.stepNote0: APS phase — Define the project scope: type of operation, footprint, municipality. These parameters determine applicable standards (municipal vs state roads, CTG 002/009).
steps.stepNote1: Lot 0-1 — Site installation and earthmoving. Earthworks typically represent 10-15% of the VRD budget. Ref.: CTG 002 (earthworks), CSDC-CT §2 (subgrade layer).
steps.stepNote2: Lot 2-3 — Pavement structure and kerbs. The structure is defined by the CSDC-CT (Ponts & Chaussées). Typical thicknesses: 6 cm BB + 5 cm GB + 20 cm GNT + 30 cm foundation.
steps.stepNote3: Lots 4-5 — WW/RW networks per CTG 009 (piping). WW diameters: DN200-400, RW: DN300-600. Retention basin mandatory for projects >0.5 ha (RGD drainage).
steps.stepNote4: Lot 6 — Combined trench (elec/telecom/gas) + street lighting. Ref.: Creos connection catalogue, POST Technologies, municipal technical regulations.
steps.stepNote5: Lots 7-8 — Green spaces, signage, street furniture. Batiprix 2026 vol. 2 for reference prices.
steps.stepNote6: Fees and ancillary studies. APS/APD/DCE phases per OAI CTA Privé. Engineering fees: 8-12% typical, free negotiation (Competition Council decision 2024).

hints.installationChantier: Access, fencing, site office, signboards. Source: current LU market
hints.prixDecapage: 3-8 €/m² -- Source: Batiprix 2026 vol. 2 (x1.20 LU coeff.), CTG 002 art. 3
hints.prixDeblais: 8-25 €/m³ -- Source: Batiprix 2026, STATEC earthworks index Oct. 2025 (-0.6%)
hints.prixRegardEU: 800-2,000 €/u -- Source: Batiprix 2026, Administration de l'eau TabAVIS-9100
hints.etudeGeo: Source: current LU market (LSC360, Schroeder & Associés)
hints.etudeHydro: Source: Administration de l'eau, RGD drainage
hints.honorairesBE: Source: OAI CTA Privé annex 3 (indicative, free negotiation since 2024)

sources.title: Sources:
sources.text: Average unit prices on the Luxembourg market 2025-2026. References: Batiprix 2026 vol. 2 (VRD, Green spaces) with LU coefficient x1.20, CTG 002 (earthworks), CTG 009 (piping), CSDC-CT (Ponts & Chaussées), STATEC construction price indices, Creos connection catalogue 2026, OAI CTA Privé annex 3. Prices generally include supply, transport and installation. Detailed quotes should be obtained from specialist contractors.

hvac.title: HVAC Simulator - Sizing and costing
hvac.subtitle: Sizing, product selection and budget estimation for heating, ventilation and air conditioning installations in Luxembourg.

energy.title: Tevaxia Energy
energy.description: 8 energy simulators: EPC impact on value, renovation ROI, energy community, EPBD 2050, HVAC, LENOZ.

steps.stepNote0: Step 1 - Building characteristics. These parameters determine thermal heat losses according to EN 12831. The design outdoor temperature ranges from -10 C (Luxembourg City) to -12 C (Ardennes).
steps.stepNote1: Simplified calculation of thermal heat losses according to EN 12831. The total required capacity includes heating and domestic hot water (DHW) production.
steps.stepNote2: Selection of the heating system from a catalogue of products available in Luxembourg. Products whose power range covers your requirement are marked Recommended.
steps.stepNote3: Sizing of heat emitters (underfloor heating, radiators) and hydraulic equipment (cylinders, circulators, valves). Price sources: Rehau, Uponor, Kermi, Purmo.
steps.stepNote4: Mechanical ventilation with heat recovery (MVHR). A double-flow MVHR with heat recovery is strongly recommended for energy renovations in Luxembourg (RGD 9 June 2021).
steps.stepNote5: Costing summary and calculation of financial aids (Klimabonus 2026, Enoprimes, VAT 3%). Enable fossil fuel replacement to maximise aids.

labels.catalogueHint: Required capacity: {kw} kW. Click on a product to select it.
labels.remplaceFossile: Replacement of a fossil fuel system

hints.niveauIsolation: Determines thermal heat losses in W/m2 according to EN 12831.
hints.region: Design outdoor temperature: -10 C (south/centre) to -12 C (Ardennes).
hints.typeSysteme: The system type determines available products and financial aids.
hints.typeEmetteur: Underfloor heating is recommended with heat pumps (low-temperature regime 35 C).
hints.surfacePlancher: Area covered by underfloor heating. Source: Rehau, Uponor (100-150 EUR/m2).
hints.nbRadiateurs: Number of radiators to install. Source: Kermi, Purmo (300-800 EUR/u LT, 200-600 EUR/u HT).
hints.ballonTampon: Recommended: approx. {defaut} L (30 L/kW). Source: france-chauffage-solaire.fr (500-3,000 EUR).
hints.ballonECS: Volume based on number of occupants. Source: 800-2,500 EUR.
hints.circulateur: High-efficiency class A circulator. Source: 300-600 EUR.
hints.typeVMC: Double-flow MVHR recommended for energy renovations (RGD 9 June 2021).
hints.remplaceFossile: Replacing an oil or gas boiler entitles you to an increased Klimabonus.
hints.retraitCuve: Average cost of oil tank removal and degassing: approx. 1,500 EUR.

calc.formuleExplication: {surface} m2 x {depWm2} W/m2 x {vitr} (glazing) x {region} (region) / 1000 = {result} kW

aides.noteTVA3: The super-reduced VAT rate of 3% applies to renovation works for residential buildings over 10 years old (article 44 amended VAT law).

refs.ref1: Klima-Agence - Energy and renovation advice Luxembourg
refs.ref2: EN 12831 - Method for calculation of thermal heat losses
refs.ref3: RGD 9 June 2021 - Energy performance of buildings (Legilux)

sources.title: Sources:
sources.text: Indicative prices for the Luxembourg market 2025-2026. Manufacturer catalogues: Viessmann, Daikin, Vaillant, Buderus, Mitsubishi, Panasonic, LG, Bosch, Hoval, Wolf, Atlantic, De Dietrich, Froling, Okofen, Windhager, Zehnder, Helios, Brink. LU distributors: Reckinger Alfred, Vanmarcke, General Technic, Sonepar, CRS, TSD. Aids: guichet.public.lu (Klimabonus 2026), klima-agence.lu (Enoprimes). Standards: EN 12831 (heat losses), RGD 9 June 2021 (energy performance). Prices generally include supply and installation. Detailed quotes should be obtained from approved installers.
